summary: A unit test failed: DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion asserted that the owner version should not have been incremented but it was, causing the Gradle :hibernate-core:test task to fail.
root_cause: The test assertion failed: "owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>" in DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion, which caused the ':hibernate-core:test' Gradle task to fail.

evidence.quote: org.opentest4j.AssertionFailedError: owner version not incremented ==> expected: <false> but was: <true>
evidence.reason: The exact assertion error and message explaining the expectation vs actual value.
Summary indicating there was exactly one failing test in the test run.
Gradle task failure caused by the failing tests.

evidence.quote: There were failing tests. See the report at: file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html
evidence.reason: Provides the test report location where detailed failure information can be inspected.

suggested_fix: Open the test report at file:///home/runner/work/hibernate-orm/hibernate-orm/hibernate-core/target/reports/tests/test/index.html to see the stacktrace and details for DbVersionTest.testCollectionVersion. Reproduce the failure locally and investigate why the owner version is being incremented (or whether the test expectation is incorrect). Then either fix the production code to prevent the unexpected version increment or update the test assertion if the behavior has intentionally changed, and re-run :hibernate-core:test.
